serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Bông snail with salted egg sauce is approximately 250 grams, often served as a sharing dish.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central, SR Legacy (FDC ID: 172052, 171287, 170425)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient profile estimated from primary components: Snails, raw (FDC ID: 172052), Egg, duck, salted, cooked (FDC ID: 171287), and Butter (FDC ID: 170425). Proportions estimated as 60% snail, 30% salted duck egg, 10% butter/seasoning for 100g. Secondary micronutrient fields set to -1 where the blended component estimate is not reliably derivable from the source entries for this specific dish preparation.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database — analogous estimate for low-carbohydrate seafood dish
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this dish contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates (1.54 g per 100 g). Snails and salted egg yolk are primarily protein and fat. Analogous to eggs and shellfish which have GI of 0 per University of Sydney GI Database. gi_category set to low per convention.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from salted egg sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish in strict moderation or avoid it, as the salted duck egg sauce contributes approximately 655 mg sodium per 100 g, which can significantly elevate blood pressure with regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate content (1.54 g per 100 g) and low glycemic index, though portion control is advised given the moderate fat and caloric density.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat and high sodium from butter and salted egg yolk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of this dish due to its saturated fat content (6.85 g per 100 g) from butter and egg yolk, combined with high sodium, both of which are risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bông snail with salted egg sauce (Vietnamese: Ốc bông sốt trứng muối) is a popular Southeast Asian seafood dish enjoyed in Singapore and Vietnam. It features fresh Bông snails — a variety of sea snail prized for their tender, slightly chewy flesh — stir-fried and generously coated in a rich, creamy, and intensely savory sauce made from salted duck egg yolks, butter, curry leaves, fresh chili, and garlic. The salted egg yolk sauce delivers a distinctive umami depth with a slightly grainy, buttery texture and a mild spicy note. The dish is typically served as a sharing plate or appetizer at seafood restaurants. Nutritionally, it is high in protein (approximately 13.6 g per 100 g) and fat (13.2 g per 100 g), with notable saturated fat from butter and egg yolk. Sodium content is high (approximately 655 mg per 100 g) due to the salted eggs, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Carbohydrate content is very low, resulting in a low glycemic index, which is favorable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes.
